title: Breitmann As An Uhlan. IV. Breitmann Takes the Town of Nancy
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O hear a wondrous shdory
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot soundet like romance,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How Breitmann mit four Uhlans
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vas dake de town of Nantz.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De Frן¿½ntschmen call it Nancy,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dey say its fery hard
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat Nancy mit her soldiers
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vas getook py gorpralן¿½s guard.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey dink id vas King Wilhelm
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ash Hans ride in de down,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und like Odin in his glorie
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gazed derriply arounן¿½.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Denn mit awfool condesenchen
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He at de Frן¿½ntschmen shtare,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und say, ן¿½Ye wretsched shildren?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Abbortez mir vodre mן¿½re!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hans mean de city Syndic,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Whom maire de Frן¿½ntschmen call;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So mit a tousand soldiers
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey ן¿½scort him to de Hall;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In de shair of shtade dey sot him,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der maire coom to pe heard,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und Hans glare at him fife minutes
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Pefore he shbeak a word.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Den in iron dones he ootered:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Ich temand que rentez fous:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Shai dreisig mille soldaten
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Bas loin lן¿½ici, barploo!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Aber tonnez-moi Champagner;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Shai an soif exdrortinaire
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Apout one douzaine cart-loads;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dann je fous laisse faire.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Denn he say to Schwackenhammer,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ His segretairן¿½ ן¿½Read
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A liddle exdra listן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of dings de army need,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dell dem in Franzן¿½sisch
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey moost shell de neetfool down
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In less dan dwendy minudes,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or, py Gott, Iן¿½ll purn de town.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Item one tousand vatches
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of purest gold so fair;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dazu fן¿½nf tousand silbern,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For de gommon soldiersן¿½ wear;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und tree dousand diamant ringן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey moost make tirectly come,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ We need dem for our schweethearts
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ven we write to em at home!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Von million cigarren
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Veן¿½ll accept ash extra boons
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For not squeezin dem seferely,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dazu dwelf tousand shboons.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Here der maire fell down in schwoonin,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Denn all dat he could say
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vas, ן¿½O mon dieu, de dieu, dieu!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Nous voilן¿½ ruinן¿½es!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ No wort der Breitmann ootered,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He only make a sgratch,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Calm and silend on de daple,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit a liddle friction match.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De maire versteh de motion,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So went him to de task
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of raisin mong de peoples
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot it vas der Breitmann ask.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So kam he mit de ringן¿½,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vind dem pooty soon;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So kam he mit de vatches,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und avery silber spoon.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot ash for de champagner,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He wept and loudly call
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat par dieu! he hadnן¿½t any,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For de Deutsch hafe troonk it all.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ja! de gorporalן¿½s guart have trinket
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Efery pottle in de down,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhile dese negotiations
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oop-stairs vere written down.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot der Breitmann sooplimely,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Like von who nodings felt,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said, ן¿½Instet of le champagner
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Nous brentirons du gelt.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ja wohl! Donnes cent mille franken,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Cן¿½est mir ?gal, you know;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Pid dem pring id in a horry,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For ן¿½Tis dime for oos to go.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der maire he pring de money,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und der Breitmann squeeze his hand,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Leb wohl, dou nople brickbat,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Herzbruder in Frankenland!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Boot it griefes my soul to larmen,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und I sympathize mit dein,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To pense of you, mon ami,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Sans le champagner wein.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere will oder Deutsch pe gomin,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und it preak mine heart to dink
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De vay deyן¿½ll bang and slang you
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ If dereן¿½s no champagne to trink!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Cela fous fera miserן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Que she ne feux bas see;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So, vollow mes gonseillן¿½s,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Et brenez mon afis.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Shai, moi, deux mille boutelles,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De meilleur dat man can ashk,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vich I will gladly sell
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Sheap as dirt ten franks a flask.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De maire look oop to heafen,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Wohl nodings could he say,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhile oud indo de mitnight
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Breitmann rode afay.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Away atown de falley,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Till noding more abbears
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot de glitter of de moonlight,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De moonlight on deir spears.
meaning:
Charles G. Leland's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Breitmann As An Uhlan. IV. Breitmann Takes the Town of Nancy"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
